text:
The2024 Summer Olympicswas off to a rollicking start in Paris on Friday, beginning with an innovativeopening ceremonyblending sports with fashion, art, politics and music. It was capped off by the long-awaited return ofГӮCeline DionГӮВ to the world stage.

But before she made her triumphant return, Paris gave the audience plenty of dazzling entertainment. For the first time in Olympic history, the opening ceremony took place not in a stadium, but on water, upon Paris' famous Seine River with 85 boats gliding down the waterway, carrying athletes from 206 nations, cheering and smiling despite the rainy weather. In keeping with tradition, Team USA's boat ГўВҖВ”comprised of 594 athletes representing 46 states ГўВҖВ” was situated near the end of the procession. The United States will be hosting the next summer Games in Los Angeles in 2028.

The festivities began with French soccer legend Zinedine Zidane carrying the Olympic torch underground to theГӮВ MГғВ©tro subway before passing it off to a group of kids. The kids come upon a mysterious and masked ferryman who takes them down the river to the ceremony. A burst of blue, white, and red smoke ГўВҖВ” reflecting the colors of the French flag ГўВҖВ” then appeared over the Seine.ГӮ

Г°ВҹВҮВ¬Г°ВҹВҮВ· From Olympia, Greece.Г°ВҹВҮВ«Г°ВҹВҮВ· To Paris, France.

Г°ВҹВ”ВҘ It's time for the Olympic flame to end its three-month journey across the French territory and enter the Opening Ceremony, but where is the Flame? Г°ВҹВ‘ВҖ#Paris2024#OpeningCeremonypic.twitter.com/x16HpA7fwo

ГўВҖВ” The Olympic Games (@Olympics)July 26, 2024



It's this masked, hooded ferryman who becomes a torchbearer and an ongoing figure in the opening ceremony narrative as they traverse Paris.ГӮВ Torch in hand, the person leapfrogged across rooftops of Parisian buildings, dodged musket fire during a reenactment of the French Revolution and passed through hallways at the Louvre Museum. The masked figure eventually discovered the Mona Lisa has been stolen, which led to an adventure to try and retrieve it. The journey included galloping across the Seine on a glimmering horse and eventually delivering the Olympic flag.

Related

Concurrent with the flotilla of athletes was a series of musical performances, starting with superstarLady Gaga,who performed a rendition ofГӮВ Zizi JeanmaireГўВҖВҷs ГўВҖВңMon Truc en Plumes," or ГўВҖВңMy Thing With Feathers.ГўВҖВқ Flanked by a group of backup dancers dressed in all black and carrying pink feather fans, a similarly befeathered Gaga sang and danced at the foot of the Seine. Following her performance, she took toher Instagramto share her thoughts.ГӮ

"I feel so completely grateful to have been asked to open the Paris@Olympics2024 this year. I am also humbled to be asked by the Olympics organizing committee to sing such a special French songГўВҖВ”a song to honor the French people and their tremendous history of art, music, and theatre," the singer wrote. "Although I am not a French artist, I have always felt a very special connection with French people and singing French music ГўВҖВ” I wanted nothing more than to create a performance that would warm the heart of France, celebrate French art and music, and on such a momentous occasion remind everyone of one of the most magical cities on earth ГўВҖВ” Paris."ГӮ

"We rented pom poms from Le Lido archiveГўВҖВ”a real French cabaret theater," Gaga explained. "We collaborated with Dior to create custom costumes, using naturally molted feathers. I studied French choreography that put a modern twist on a French classic. I rehearsed tirelessly to study a joyful French dance, brushing up on some old skills ГўВҖВ” I bet you didnГўВҖВҷt know I used to dance at a 60ГўВҖВҷs French party on the lower east side when I was first starting out! I hope you love this performance as much as I do. And to everyone in France, thank you so much for welcoming me to your country to sing in honor of you ГўВҖВ” itГўВҖВҷs a gift IГўВҖВҷll never forget! Congratulations to all the athletes who are competing in this yearГўВҖВҷs Olympic Games! It is my supreme honor to sing for you and cheer you on!! Watching the Olympic Games always makes me cry! Your talent is unimaginable. Let the games begin!"

https://www.instagram.com/p/C95VospTkcE/?img_index=1

French metal band Gojira delivered one of the most energeticperformancesof the ceremony ГўВҖВ” mixing music from the musical "Les Miserables," rock-centric sounds and classical opera ГўВҖВ” fromГӮВ the Conciergerie, the residence of French kings in medieval times. The band stood on ledges outside the windows of the building while pyrotechnics lit up the foreground. Other apertures were filled with images of the famously guillotined French QueenMarie Antoinetteholding her severed head. The segment was notably kicked off by the ringing of Notre Dame's bells, marking the first time the bells have sounded sinceГӮa 2019 fire ravaged the cathedral.ГӮ

Aya Nakamura! One of FranceГўВҖВҷs greatest pop stars, and the orchestra of the Republican Guard performing on the Pont des Arts.

This moment celebrates the unifying power of the French language, influenced by diverse communities across time.#Paris2024#OpeningCeremonypic.twitter.com/QYlS3EOD3G

ГўВҖВ” The Olympic Games (@Olympics)July 26, 2024



French-Malian singer Aya Nakamura was another headliner who lent her talents to the ceremony from a bridge across from the Seine. Dressed in a gilded feather outfit, the singer performed a version of her song, "Djadja" on a bridge across the Seine.

In a separate emotional tribute, Juliette Armanet sang "Imagine" by John Lennon and Yoko Ono ГўВҖВ” a song that has been performed at numerous Olympic ceremonies since the 1996 Atlanta, Georgia Games ГўВҖВ” atop a floating barge next to pianistГӮВ Sofiane Pamart playing a piano engulfed in flames. As the song concluded, NBC displayed a message: "We stand and call for peace."

Г°ВҹВҢВҸГ°ВҹВӨВҚAnd the world will live as one.

ItГўВҖВҷs always a powerful moment when ГўВҖВңImagineГўВҖВқ is performed at the Olympic Games#OpeningCeremony.#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/kIpeLgA8Gx

ГўВҖВ” The Olympic Games (@Olympics)July 26, 2024



Among the final torchbearers of the evening was Charles Coste, the oldest living French Olympic medalist. Asnoted by NBC,Coste is a centenarian born in 1924 ГўВҖВ” the last time France hosted the Olympics (before the Games were split between Summer and Winter). He earned his country a gold medal in 1948 when he won the team pursuit cycling event in London.ГӮ

The evening culminated with the moment so many fans have been waiting for,ГӮCeline Dion's first public performance since halting her tour amid her struggles with Stiff Person Syndrome (SPS). Dion was first diagnosed with the rare and progressive neurological disorder in 2022 and has battled a litany of physical symptoms that have inhibited powerhouse vocal abilities from operating as usual. In June, Dion appeared before a New York audience at a screening of"I Am: Celine Dion,"an intimate documentary that revealed the depth of her health plight.ГӮ

This was the second time the French-Canadian singer had headlined at the Olympic opening ceremony. Nearly 20 years ago, Dion sang her song,"The Power of the Dream,"at the 1996 Summer Games.

From the platform midway up the iconic Eiffel Tower, Dion ГўВҖВ” wearing a diamond-encrusted gown with glittering fringe ГўВҖВ” exuded strength and resilience as she belted out Edith Piaf'sГӮВ "Hymne ГғВ  lГўВҖВҷamour," or "Hymn to Love." Arms outstretched to Olympians and her fans alike, Dion at several points during the song appeared to become emotional. She never faltered, however, and as her voice soared in the night, the Eiffel Tower ГўВҖВ”ГӮВ embossed with the five interlaced Olympic rings ГўВҖВ”ГӮВ glittered in the background.

Immediately following Dion's performance, singer Kelly Clarkson ГўВҖВ” who, along with former NFL player Peyton Manning and sports commentator Mike Tirico served as NBC's broadcast team for the ceremony ГўВҖВ” was emotionally overwhelmed.ГӮГўВҖВңPeople donГўВҖВҷt know her story, what sheГўВҖВҷs been going through physically," Clarkson eventually said.

ГўВҖВңSheГўВҖВҷs a vocal athlete ГўВҖВ“ sheГўВҖВҷs incredible.ГўВҖВқ
